channel.title: Signing and Verification on Open Component Model
channel.description: Recent content in Signing and Verification on Open Component Model

item.title: Plain Signatures
item.description: In this tutorial, you’ll sign a component version with a private key and verify it with the corresponding public key. By the end, you’ll understand the complete signing and verification workflow that ensures component authenticity and integrity.

item.title: Certificate Chains (PEM)
item.description: PEM signing embeds an X.509 certificate chain directly in the signature value, letting verifiers pin a root CA rather than a bare public key. Use it when your organization has existing PKI infrastructure.
